paper_definition_large_deviation_rate.statement: For a real sequence A indexed by sample size and a real number r, paper_definition_large_deviation_rate A r is exactly the paper large-deviation-rate predicate definition_large_deviation_rate A r.

source_proposition1_thm_consistency_tier.statement: For a finite ranking law and staged target prefix sets, assuming every stage has a cross-tier pair and no cross-tier top-prefix probabilities tie, strict top-prefix dominance of every target member over every nonmember at every cut is equivalent to every reasonable prefix-score rule learning the staged target sets, meaning the selection error probability tends to 0.

source_proposition2_thm_pairwiselearning.statement: For a finite signal law and two score functions with nonnegative expected score gap, the pairwise scoring error probability either has exponential rate pairwiseScoringRate, or has exponential rate -log pZero for a positive zero-gap probability pZero, or is eventually zero.

source_proposition3_lem_pairwiselearning.statement: For finite ternary approval score gaps taking values 1, 0, and -1 with probabilities pUp, pZero, and pDown and pDown <= pUp, the pairwise scoring error probability either has exponential rate approvalPairwiseRate pUp pDown or is eventually zero.

source_proposition4_thm_goal_learning_fi.statement: For finitely many relevant pairs under a finite signal law, if every pair has nonnegative expected score gap and each pair has a positive nonnegative weight, then the weighted sum of the pairwise finite-score-gap error probabilities has some extended exponential rate.

source_theorem1_lem_randomizebetterscori.statement: For any finite randomized family of reasonable prefix scoring rules whose weights are nonnegative and sum to 1, if the target winner set satisfies strict top-prefix dominance, then the weight-averaged static scoring rule is reasonable, learns the winner set with error probability tending to 0, and has a static finite outcome-learning rate at least as large as the randomized scoring rule rate.

source_theorem2_lem_randomizenotbetterap.statement: For any finite randomized K-approval rule with nonnegative weights summing to 1 and any candidate pair, there exists a static component rule whose extended approval pairwise rate weakly dominates the randomized rule pairwise rate.

source_theorem_lem_randomizebetterapprov.statement: In the constructed four-candidate W-selection ranking law, strict top-prefix dominance holds for the relevant cross-tier pairs, and the 50/50 randomized approval rule has finite outcome-learning rate strictly larger than the best static K-approval cutoff among the finite static family.

source_corollary_lem_mallowsnorando.statement: For a finite Mallows model with q < 1 and a target W candidate, any finite randomized family of nontrivial K-approval rules is weakly dominated by some static proper-prefix cutoff, and that cutoff is optimal among static cutoffs for the Mallows top-W K-approval outcome rate.

source_theorem_lem_mallowsnotWK_countere.statement: There exists a high-noise Mallows instance centered at the identity in the four-candidate setting such that the W-approval cutoff is not approval-rate optimal: some different cutoff has strictly larger finite outcome-learning rate.
